message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Purpose</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    The angiotensin converting enzyme 2 (ACE2) plays a regulatory role in the cardiovascular system and serves SARS-CoV-2 as an entry receptor. The aim of this study was to synthesize and evaluate radiofluorinated derivatives of the ACE2 inhibitor MLN-4760. [
                    <jats:sup>18</jats:sup>
                    F]F-MLN-4760 and [
                    <jats:sup>18</jats:sup>
                    F]F-Aza-MLN-4760 were demonstrated to be suitable for non-invasive imaging of ACE2, potentially enabling a better understanding of its expression dynamics.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Based on computational molecular modeling, the ACE2-binding modes of F-MLN-4760 and F-Aza-MLN-4760 were similar to that of MLN-4760. Co-crystallization of the hACE2/F-MLN-4760 protein complex was performed for confirmation. Displacement experiments using [
                    <jats:sup>3</jats:sup>
                    H]MLN-4760 enabled the determination of the binding affinities of the synthesized F-MLN-4760 and F-Aza-MLN-4760 to ACE2 expressed in HEK-ACE2 cells. Aryl trimethylstannane-based and pyridine-based radiofluorination precursors were synthesized and used for the preparation of the respective radiotracers. [
                    <jats:sup>18</jats:sup>
                    F]F-MLN-4760 and [
                    <jats:sup>18</jats:sup>
                    F]F-Aza-MLN-4760 were evaluated with regard to the uptake in HEK-ACE2 and HEK-ACE cells and in vitro binding to tissue sections of HEK-ACE2 xenografts and normal organs of mice. Biodistribution and PET/CT imaging studies of [
                    <jats:sup>18</jats:sup>
                    F]F-MLN-4760 and [
                    <jats:sup>18</jats:sup>
                    F]F-Aza-MLN-4760 were performed using HEK-ACE2 and HEK-ACE xenografted nude mice.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Crystallography data revealed an equal ACE2-binding mode for F-MLN-4760 as previously found for MLN-4760 and indicated that the same would hold true for F-Aza-MLN-4760. The IC
                    <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                    values were all in the high nM range, but three-fold lower for F-MLN-4760 and seven-fold lower for F-Aza-MLN-4760 than for MLN-4760. [
                    <jats:sup>18</jats:sup>
                    F]F-MLN-4760 and [
                    <jats:sup>18</jats:sup>
                    F]F-Aza-MLN-4760 were obtained in 1.4 ± 0.3 GBq and 0.5 ± 0.1 GBq activity with >99% radiochemical purity in a 5.3% and 1.2% radiochemical yield, respectively. Uptake in HEK-ACE2 cells was higher for [
                    <jats:sup>18</jats:sup>
                    F]F-MLN-4760 (67 ± 9%) than for [
                    <jats:sup>18</jats:sup>
                    F]F-Aza-MLN-4760 (37 ± 8%) after 3 h incubation while negligible uptake was seen in HEK-ACE cells (<0.3%). [
                    <jats:sup>18</jats:sup>
                    F]F-MLN-4760 and [
                    <jats:sup>18</jats:sup>
                    F]F-Aza-MLN-4760 accumulated specifically in HEK-ACE2 xenografts of mice (13 ± 2% IA/g and 15 ± 2% IA/g at 1 h p.i.) with almost no uptake observed in HEK-ACE xenografts (<0.3% IA/g). This was confirmed by PET/CT imaging, which also visualized unspecific accumulation in the gall bladder and intestinal tract.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Both radiotracers showed specific and selective binding to ACE2 in vitro and in vivo. [
                    <jats:sup>18</jats:sup>
                    F]F-MLN-4760 was, however, obtained in higher yields and the ACE2-binding affinity was superior over that of [
                    <jats:sup>18</jats:sup>
                    F]F-Aza-MLN-4760. [
                    <jats:sup>18</jats:sup>
                    F]F-MLN-4760 would, thus, be the candidate of choice for further developlment to enable PET imaging of ACE2 in patients.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Development of radiofluorinated MLN-4760 derivatives for PET imaging of the SARS-CoV-2 entry receptor ACE2

reference.article-title: Mapping COVID-19 with nuclear imaging: from infection to functional sequelae
reference.journal-title: Am J Nucl Med Mol Imaging

reference.unstructured: Ding J , Zhang Q , Jiang J , Zhou N , Yu Z , Wang Z , et al. Preclinical Evaluation and Pilot Clinical Study of Al18F-DX600-BCH for non-invasive PET Mapping of Angiotensin Converting Enzyme 2 in Mammal. J Nucl Med. 2023;64.
